key_insight: Length penalties on incorrect answers can destabilize GRPO, so efficiency training is safer when brevity rewards are applied only to correct completions and controlled adaptively.
engineering_relevance: ACOER offers a more stable way to reduce reasoning cost in large models, improving accuracy while cutting token usage by over 60% across math benchmarks.
method: Use correct-only rewards to avoid penalizing wrong answers repeatedly.
Add dynamic budget normalization to prevent over-compression.
Apply control-loop penalty adjustments to stabilize training over time.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a systematic evaluation across diverse mathematical reasoning benchmarks, showing improved accuracy over the base model with more than 60% fewer generated tokens.
watch_item: Adaptive reward control may be sensitive to budget tuning and training dynamics, so miscalibration could still cause efficiency collapse or reduced reasoning quality.
paper_title: Beyond Penalizing Mistakes: Stabilizing Efficiency Training in Large Reasoning Models via Adaptive Correct-Only Rewards
